---
marimo-static-output: true
---

# Mask { #json2vec.Mask }

```python
Mask(**data)
```

Structured masking policy attached to an `Array`.

`Mask` selects candidate coordinates inside the owning array, samples from those
coordinates with either `rate` or `count`, and applies the selection to active
descendant leaves unless `exclude` removes them.

See [Dynamic Masking](../core-concepts/dynamic-masking.qmd) for examples and
coordinate rules.

## Attributes

| Name | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `name` | `None` | Optional name for the mask policy. Names must be unique within one array when provided. |
| `rate` | `None` | Bernoulli probability for each candidate slot. Mutually exclusive with `count`. |
| `count` | `None` | Exact number of candidate slots sampled per parent coordinate, capped by available candidates. Mutually exclusive with `rate`. |
| `window` | `None` | Candidate span length. `None` means all slots in the selected coordinate space. |
| `array` | `False` | Use fixed padded array coordinates instead of observed non-padded coordinates. |
| `start` | `False` | Take the candidate window from the start instead of the end. |
| `offset` | `0` | Skip slots away from the selected edge before taking the window. |
| `exclude` | `None` | One predicate, or a list of predicates, for descendant leaves that should not receive this mask. |

## Validation

Exactly one of `rate` or `count` is required. `rate` must be between `0` and
`1`; `count` must be non-negative; `window` must be positive when provided; and
`offset` must be non-negative.
